hero.title: About Ombaka Go
hero.subtitle: Promoting tourism and sustainable development in Benguela

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: To position Benguela as a leading tourist destination in Angola and the region through an integrated digital platform that connects visitors, local communities, and investors, promoting sustainable development and enhancing the province's natural and cultural heritage.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: To be recognized as the leading tourism promotion platform in Angola, contributing to making Benguela one of the main tourist destinations in Southern Africa by 2030, with a dynamic, inclusive, and sustainable tourism sector that benefits the entire community.

about.title: What is Ombaka Go?
about.p1: Ombaka Go is the official digital tourism promotion platform of Benguela Province, Angola. Developed to boost sustainable tourism and regional economic development, the platform offers a complete experience for visitors, investors, and industry partners.
about.p2: Through Ombaka Go, visitors can discover the 10 municipalities of Benguela, explore over 100 tourist zones, learn about cultural events, find investment opportunities, and connect with certified tour operators.
about.p3: The platform integrates modern technology with Benguela's rich cultural heritage, offering advanced search tools, interactive maps, an event calendar, and a partner system that connects visitors with the best services in the region.
about.p4: "Ombaka" means "rain" in Umbundu, the native language of the region, symbolizing abundance, renewal, and prosperity — core values that guide our mission to promote sustainable tourism growth in Benguela.

sustainability.title: Sustainability
sustainability.description: Promoting responsible tourism that preserves Benguela's natural and cultural heritage.

climate.title: Climate
climate.details: Dry season: May to October | Rainy season: November to April

visa.question: Do I need a visa to visit Benguela?
visa.answer: Yes, most international visitors need a visa to enter Angola. It is recommended to obtain the visa before traveling through the Angolan embassy or consulate in your country. There are also visa-on-arrival options at the airport for some countries.

bestTime.question: What is the best time to visit Benguela?
bestTime.answer: The best time is during the dry season, from May to October, when temperatures are milder and there is less rain. This period is ideal for outdoor activities and beach visits. The average temperature ranges from 20°C to 28°C.

safety.question: Is it safe to travel to Benguela?
safety.answer: Benguela is considered a relatively safe province in Angola. As with any tourist destination, it's important to take basic precautions: avoid displaying valuables, stay alert in busy areas, and don't carry large amounts of cash. At night, prefer well-lit areas.

currencyExchange.question: Where can I exchange money?
currencyExchange.answer: You can exchange money at banks, authorized exchange offices, and some hotels. The main banks are located in downtown Benguela and Lobito. ATMs are available in major cities, but it's recommended to carry cash for more remote areas.

vaccines.question: What vaccines are required?
vaccines.answer: Yellow fever vaccine is mandatory for entry into Angola. It is also recommended to be up-to-date with hepatitis A and B, tetanus, typhoid fever vaccines, and depending on the region you plan to visit, malaria prophylaxis may be advisable. Consult a doctor before traveling.

transportation.question: How do I get around between municipalities?
transportation.answer: Several options are available: taxis for city trips, candongueiros (collective public transport) for short to medium distances, and intercity buses for longer distances. For more comfort and flexibility, consider renting a car with a driver.
